features.description: Investigate the error source and locate problematic code
features.details: Search the codebase for all instances where '.head' is accessed on a PullRequest object. Review the GitHub API client library documentation to understand the correct PullRequest object structure. Identify the exact file(s) and line numbers where the error occurs.

Examine issue #117 to understand what operation was being attempted when the failure occurred. This will provide context on what the automation was trying to accomplish and help identify the correct fix.

Update the code to use the correct attribute name for accessing pull request head information. In the GitHub API, this is typically accessed via 'head.ref' or through a different object structure. Implement proper null checks and error handling.

features.description: Add unit tests for PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Create unit tests that mock the PullRequest object and verify correct attribute access. Include tests for edge cases like missing attributes and API response variations.

features.description: Audit codebase for similar attribute access patterns
features.details: Search for other potentially incorrect attribute accesses on GitHub API objects (Issues, PullRequests, Repositories, etc.) that could cause similar failures. Document and fix any issues found.

features.description: Add defensive error handling and logging
features.details: Implement try-catch blocks around GitHub API object attribute access with detailed logging. This will help identify future issues more quickly and prevent complete workflow failures.

features.description: Close or update issue #121 after fix is verified
features.details: Once the fix is deployed and tested, close this issue with a summary of the fix applied. If issue #117 still needs processing, trigger a retry of the automation.

overall_approach: Investigate the automation codebase to locate where the 'head' attribute is being accessed on a PullRequest object, determine the correct attribute name or API structure, and implement a fix with proper error handling and tests.
potential_risks: The fix may require changes to multiple files if the incorrect attribute usage is widespread
There may be other similar attribute access issues in the codebase that haven't surfaced yet
Changes to the automation code could affect other automated workflows
Without proper tests, the same issue could recur with different GitHub API objects
